message:
feat(fcp-core): enrich auth configured summary and add descriptor stability tests

Enhance AuthDescriptor::configured() to differentiate between
secret-reference-backed and direct auth configurations in the summary
text, providing clearer guidance on how the connector's credentials are
managed.

Add 8 comprehensive tests validating descriptor behavior in edge cases:
- auth_descriptor_without_advertised_caps_stays_unknown: verifies
  unknown state when no auth capabilities are advertised
- auth_descriptor_not_yet_measured_has_stable_json_shape: ensures the
  NotYetMeasured variant produces a predictable JSON structure
- auth_descriptor_configured_replaces_pending_summary: confirms that
  configuring auth replaces the initial pending summary text
- prerequisite_catalog_not_yet_measured_has_stable_json_shape: JSON
  shape stability for prerequisite catalogs in unmeasured state
- readiness_descriptor_not_yet_measured_becomes_unavailable_with_unhealthy_runtime:
  validates health state interaction when runtime goes unhealthy
- readiness_descriptor_not_yet_measured_has_stable_json_shape: JSON
  shape stability for readiness descriptors
- connector_descriptor_overall_status_prefers_unavailable_over_unknown_and_not_yet_measured:
  validates status precedence ordering across surfaces

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
